description: A seafood dish centered on scallops, often quickly seared so the exterior browns while the interior remains tender. Scallops have a delicate, slightly sweet flavor and are used in many cuisines.
preparation_steps: Pat the scallops dry and season lightly.
Heat a pan until very hot.
Sear the scallops briefly on each side until browned but still tender.
Finish with butter, lemon, or sauce if desired.

nutrition.fat: Low, unless rich sauce is added

health_advice.benefits: High in protein and generally lean when simply cooked.
health_advice.risks: Rich sauces can increase saturated fat, and shellfish allergies are a concern for some people.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use simple preparation with lemon, herbs, and moderate fat to preserve the delicate texture and keep the dish lighter.

dietary_notes.note: Naturally gluten-free when not breaded and when sauces are gluten-free.

allergens: Possible dairy if butter or cream sauce is used

serving_info.typical_serving: Several scallops as an appetizer or main plate portion
